fact: October 15 is exactly 288 days into the year - that's almost 80% of the whole year gone!
In the Northern Hemisphere, leaves are falling so fast that a single maple tree can drop 200,000 leaves by this date!
The sun is setting about 2 minutes earlier each day around October 15, making bedtime feel like it comes super early.
Squirrels are in total panic mode right now - they're burying up to 25 nuts per day to prepare for winter!
October 15 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, which means the zodiac scales are perfectly balanced - just like the autumn equinox that just passed!
Spider webs covered in morning dew become super visible around this time because the air gets much more humid as temperatures drop.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 15 is prime baby animal season - lambs, calves, and chicks are being born everywhere!
The moon looks extra huge and orange when it rises around this date because of all the particles floating in the crisp autumn air.
Monarch butterflies are flying over 100 miles per day right now on their epic journey to Mexico - some might be passing overhead today!
October 15 is roughly when many deciduous trees hit their peak fall colors because chlorophyll breaks down fastest when days and nights are almost equal length.
Bears are eating up to 20,000 calories per day around this date - that's like eating 40 hamburgers every single day!
The air pressure changes so much in mid-October that some people can actually feel storms coming in their bones and joints.
